English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Stuyvesant Morris Pell papers
Pell, Stuyvesant Morris, 1905-1943.
Pell, Stuyvesant Morris, 1905-1943. (Title of work: Scribblings of an outdoor boy..)
3459
4.4 cubic feet. 4.4 cubic feet.
8 mapcase folders. 8 mapcase folders.
[ca.1917-1945]. {Manuscripts, research materials Includes lab and lecture notes taken by Pell while at Cornell University, sketches and drawings, thesis and monographs. Collection material in English 2068242
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
May 2013
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, May 2013
Date modified:
Jude Corina, February 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Stuyvesant Morris Pell was a specialist in conservation of natural resources and manager of the 16,000 acre range of the Huron Mountain Club in northern Michigan. He was born in New York City and received a B.S. from Cornell in 1939 and an M.S. in 1941. For five years he was warden of the Pleasant Valley Bird and Wild Flower Sanctuary in Lenox, Massachusetts, and he spent one summer as a naturalist and ranger in Acadia National Park, Bar Harbor, Maine.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes lab and lecture notes taken by Pell while at Cornell University with sketches and drawings related to various courses; thesis, monographs, and notes on the common snapping turtle, ca.1939; notebooks with photographs and commentary on wildlife including a "Log of the Pleasant Valley Beaver Colony," 1932, "Log of the Survey of Southern Sanctuaries," 1937, and others from Yosemite Field School, 1939, and from Acadia National Park, Bar Harbor, Maine, 1940; broadsides, brochures, and pamphlets relating to wildlife sanctuaries; prepared biology slides, dissecting kit, and field magnifying lens; original manuscript, carbon copies, and galley proof of SCRIBBLINGS OF AN OUTDOOR BOY, an autobiography; sketches and sketchbooks, pen-and-ink drawings and original watercolor paintings, mostly of birds, wildlife, and camping scenes; also glass slides and photographs of Pell's watercolors, scenes, trips, and wildlife.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Student Papers Box 1 and Map case Coursework Lecture Notes Diplomas and Certificates
Series II. Field Work Boxes 1-2, 4 and Mapcase Folder 7 Field Notes and Notebooks Informational Materials
Series III. Manuscript and Reprints Boxes 3 and 4
Series IV. Photographic Materials Boxes 4-7
Series V. Artwork Box 4, 9, 10 and Mapcase Folder 1-6 and 8-9
Series VI. Scientific and Art Objects Box 3 and 8
Cite As:
Stuyvesant Morris Pell papers, #3459. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Paintings. Autobiographies. Drawings. Sketches. Lenox (Mass.). Bar Harbor (Me.). Naturalists. Conservationists. Birds in art. Birds Ornithological illustration. Orithology. Wildlife conservation. Conservation of natural resources. Huron Mountain Club Pleasant Valley Bird and Wild Flower Sanctuary Acadia National Park Yosemite Field School

Scope and Contents
167 plants, birds, and countryside scenes. For both Box 5 and 6 many of these sides were issued by the Department of Public Education, Museum of Natural History (NYC). Others bear the name of Edward Van Altena, 71-79 W. 45th Street, NYC.4"x3.25" glass plate slides635mm color slides6Pell duck banding71Dr. Loncope Housatonic River72Sanctuary scenes73Sanctuary Ducks74Sanctuary Birds75Sanctuary Beaver76Lenox trips77Sanctuary pictures (except apartment)78Beaver drawings for children's story79Sanctuary710Duck backgrounds and watercolors711Lecture Slides712Pittsfield trout hatchery713Charleston with W.P.1935714Charleston, with K.L.W.1936715Southern Trip - Birds, Great White Heron, Mallards1937716Southern Trip - Refuge Work1937717Southern Trip - Soil Erosion1937718Southern Trip - Ducktown1937719Southern Trip - Various1937720Penikese Island and Ithaca1938721Applied Ornithology722Trips - Cape May, Sweet Briar, and Yosemite1938-1939723Negatives for Pell High trip724Negatives for Yosemite Valley Research Reserve725Yosemite trips out726Yosemite trips727Arcadia1940728Negatives for Huron Mountain729Negatives for Huron Mountain730Huron Mountain731Series V.
